title: Is your store's music driving customers out the door? — Daniel Fox
meta_description: Most retailers assume any music beats silence. The research disagrees. Mismatched music, and loud music, measurably shorten how long customers stay — and shorter visits mean smaller baskets.
schema: <script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Article","headline":"Is your store's music driving customers out the door?","description":"How mismatched and overly loud music shortens dwell time and pushes customers to leave faster.","author":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox","alternateName":"Daniel Fox","jobTitle":"Retail Music Behaviorist","url":"https://danielchristopherfox.com"},"publisher":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox"},"mainEntityOfPage":"https://danielchristopherfox.com/notes/is-your-music-driving-customers-away.html","about":["Retail music","Dwell time","Store atmosphere","Consumer behavior"]}</script>
